title: Belosynapsis ciliata
intro: Belosynapsis ciliata, also known as Belosynapsis, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as wounds and infections. Bioactive constituents of Belosynapsis include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Belosynapsis is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are employed to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Belosynapsis ciliata is a genus of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant is native to Africa and is part of the order Asterales. It is also known as the ciliate synapsis. Further classification of Belosynapsis ciliata includes the kingdom Plantae and the class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification is further refined with its family Campanulaceae being part of the superfamily Asteroideae.
uses: Belosynapsis ciliata has been traditionally used in some parts of the world for its potential medicinal properties.
